name: James Bond
description: Agent 007 — actors, villains, gadgets, and films

questions.q: What is James Bond's iconic agent number?

source.notes: The '00' prefix signifies a licence to kill.

questions.q: James Bond works for which British intelligence agency?

source.notes: Officially the Secret Intelligence Service (SIS).

questions.q: How does James Bond famously prefer his Martini?

source.notes: First uttered in 'Dr. No' (1962).

questions.q: Which Scottish actor was the first to portray James Bond on the big screen, in 1962's 'Dr. No'?

source.notes: Connery played Bond in six official films.

questions.q: What luxury British sports car brand is most associated with James Bond?

source.notes: First appeared as the DB5 in 'Goldfinger' (1964).

questions.q: Which British author created James Bond in a series of novels starting with 'Casino Royale' in 1953?

source.notes: Fleming wrote 12 Bond novels and two short-story collections.

questions.q: In what year did the first James Bond film, 'Dr. No', premiere?

source.notes: Released October 5, 1962 in the UK.

questions.q: Pierce Brosnan made his Bond debut in which 1995 film?

source.notes: Brosnan played Bond in four films (1995–2002).

questions.q: Bond's recurring arch-nemesis Ernst Stavro Blofeld is famously pictured stroking what kind of animal?

source.notes: Head of the criminal organization SPECTRE.

questions.q: Which actor played James Bond in the 2006 'Casino Royale' reboot?

source.notes: Craig played Bond in five films (2006–2021).

questions.q: How many Bond films did Timothy Dalton headline in his tenure as 007?

source.notes: 'The Living Daylights' (1987) and 'Licence to Kill' (1989).

questions.q: Which actor played James Bond in the most official Eon Productions films (7)?

source.notes: From 'Live and Let Die' (1973) to 'A View to a Kill' (1985).

questions.q: In the Bond universe, who is the gadget-master who supplies 007 with his inventive tools?

source.notes: Short for 'Quartermaster'; played memorably by Desmond Llewelyn and Ben Whishaw.

questions.q: What is the single-letter name of Bond's boss, the head of MI6?

source.notes: Played by Judi Dench for seven films from 'GoldenEye' through 'Skyfall'.

questions.q: Miss Moneypenny serves as secretary to which Bond character?

source.notes: Known for her witty flirtations with Bond.

questions.q: Which Danish actor played the villain Le Chiffre opposite Daniel Craig in 'Casino Royale' (2006)?

source.notes: Mikkelsen famously plays the poker match opposite Bond.

questions.q: The James Bond theme song was composed by which English musician?

source.notes: Though John Barry arranged the iconic version, Norman is credited as composer.

questions.q: In which 1967 Bond film does 007 infiltrate a SPECTRE hideout inside a hollowed-out volcano in Japan?

source.notes: Screenplay by Roald Dahl.

questions.q: What is the name of the global criminal organization that Blofeld heads in the Bond films?

source.notes: Special Executive for Counter-intelligence, Terrorism, Revenge, and Extortion.

questions.q: Which 2021 film was Daniel Craig's final outing as James Bond?

source.notes: Directed by Cary Joji Fukunaga.

questions.q: As of 'No Time to Die' (2021), how many films are in the official Eon Productions James Bond series?

source.notes: Not counting the 1967 'Casino Royale' spoof or the 1983 Connery return 'Never Say Never Again'.

questions.q: Which Australian actor is the only person to play James Bond in a single official film — 'On Her Majesty's Secret Service' (1969)?

source.notes: Lazenby reportedly turned down a contract for more films.
